mentions.note: $7.5B contract notwithstanding, company making no money in market where peers are profitable; rotation away from losers to winners.
AWS grew 28% (insanely strong); Andy Jassy's division proving doubters wrong; may need more power/compute for data center work; fantastic cloud business.
Fuel cells for data centers in short supply with no dirty emissions; jumped 27% on supply constraint narrative—one of Cramer's absolute favorites.
Up 100% YTD and 500% since mid-2025; despite great run, valuation too hot to chase at these levels after 100%+ gain.
Missed quarter despite CEO Russell Weiner's execution; no catalysts on conference call; other restaurant names (Starbucks, Brinker, YUM) performing better; don't want for yield.

20 consecutive quarters SSS growth; new chicken sandwich 80% bigger than competitor, priced at $10.99 in three-for-me menu; fighting shrinkflation with value and portion expansion; April off to strong start.
Master limited partnership; better growth-and-income alternative to AT&T; performed well during war and before.
'Science drive-by,' not a real company; avoid entirely; sell if held.
Hold; nothing special, weak profitability, small dividend; no reason to buy or sell.
Maintenance stock; sell off when air travel weak (like now) is buying opportunity. People worried about air travel reduce maintenance demand, but good quarter reported today; buy the pullback.
Backup energy for data centers in short supply; hyperscaler orders driving stock higher; shortage of energy for data centers real constraint.
Crushed Q1 in every line; Google Cloud extraordinary, YouTube and Search incredibly strong, funding semiconductor buildout; one of two winners in Magnificent Seven.
CPU shortage crippling AI agent growth; stock rallied 12% as constrained supply is exactly what market rewards in fourth industrial revolution.
New casino name unfamiliar to Cramer; needs research before recommendation; family-owned Atlantis/Monarch properties in Reno and Black Hawk with strong rewards program.
Raised capex by $10B despite 33% revenue growth—red flag; fastest revenue growth in 5 years but CapEx hike without clear justification concerning; don't buy.
Only rare earth name Cramer really blesses; government backing, solid management, performing well with government support.
Disappointing quarter relative to expectations; software play lacks excitement vs. shortage plays; could consider on pullback if dividend-laden, but Cramer prefers Ford.
Auto chip shortage driving 25% gain; once albatross, now must-have as cars become software-defined; supply constraint story intact.

Beat + raised guidance; Brian Niccol turnaround fixing service and hospitality culture first, margins will recover to 13%+ from 10%; loyalty program stagnant until now (pre-order rolling out); stock up 8% today, more upside as analysts upgrade on margin recovery.

41% revenue growth, 18 consecutive quarters beating golden rule of 40; no guidance raise due to zero Fed rate cuts priced in; strong non-lending revenue growth ($390M) and Trump account opportunity; underlying trends robust despite stock selloff.
Disk drive maker can't produce fast enough; supply-constrained story the market wants in data center buildout environment.
Income without growth; prefer master limited partnerships like Enterprise Products (EPD) or other MLPs with better growth-plus-income profile.
Hold long-term with CEO Torstein Hagen; stock at $78 feels like $100; don't trade it, own it.

mentions.company: Western Alliance Bancorporation (implied WANO MLP context unclear—likely Enterprise Products energy MLP reference)
mentions.note: Alternative to AT&T with growth and income; Cramer prefers over telecom dividend.
IPO priced above range; speculative but high-reward nuclear SMR + fuel play with Amazon backing, DoE support, $1.4B+ cash. Only buy with loss-tolerant capital given 2–3 year path to scale.
+8% same-store sales growth via Niccol disciples' superior speed, customer experience, and loyalty program; amazing quarter; disciples of Brian driving execution.
